mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate elevated above 2.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations. This compression of present value of future corporate earnings produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 3.7% indicates tight labor market with wage pressure and inflation persistence, forcing continued monetary tightening that depresses equity valuations. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%) when UNRATE < 3.7%, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and recessionary pressure. This demand destruction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances following RSAFS declines > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while inflation remains elevated, signaling stagflationary pressure. This supply-side constraint with persistent inflation produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 7%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of stagflationary instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows no significant interaction term (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1500K units annualized, signaling collapse in residential investment demand. This demand shock propagates through construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of instances when HOUST < 1500, or Rank IC between HOUST and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month while unemployment remains below 4%, signaling labor market cooling without recession. This soft-landing signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as recession fears ease.
regime_hint: soft-landing scenario with labor market normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 48% of soft-landing instances, or the interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE level is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth decelerates below 8% while federal funds rate remains above 2.0%, signaling peak inflation and imminent monetary policy pivot. This expectation of future rate cuts produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: peak inflation with policy normalization expectations
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of peak-inflation instances, or the joint signal of declining inflation and elevated rates shows no significant positive predictive power (Rank IC < 0.04).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or declining, signaling recessionary demand destruction. This dual demand weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0.5%
regime_hint: recession or near-recession regime with demand collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of weak-demand instances, or the interaction between GDP growth and retail sales shows no significant joint effect (p > 0.18) in predicting forward returns.
